AMD.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 2026 earnings on May 5, 2026 (after close) — approximately 3 trading days away. Binary catalyst is real and imminent, but the technical setup going in is exhausted, not constructive. Net news sentiment: mixed. Positive catalysts include DA Davidson upgrade to Buy with $375 PT (April 29), AMD +76% in April hitting ATH of ~$353, Meta $60B infrastructure deal, TSMC 2nm first-mover advantage, and CEO Lisa Su reaffirming the long-term AI Data Center thesis. Negative signals include Northland downgrade to Market Perform ($260 PT) citing stretched valuation, stock already trading at or above several bull targets (Evercore ISI $358), beat-and-lower risk on Q2 guidance given 25% tariff headwind on AI chips, and 2:1 insider selling ratio with CEO Su selling ~$16M in March 2026. Insider activity: net selling — bearish lean. Technical setup quality: severely extended. Daily RSI-14 = 83.06, RSI-21 = 86.46 (deeply overbought); ADX = 74.94 (parabolic trend); 52.6% above 50-SMA; 21-day ROC = +74.26%; Z-score 50 = 2.6; Bollinger pct_b = 0.93 (pressing upper band). Hourly ADX = 15.89 (weak intraday trend despite price at highs); hourly relative volume = 0.67 — below average, no evidence of informed accumulation ahead of earnings. Lessons context: a near-identical stretched RSI (81-87) catalyst setup on NVDA resulted in -$264.65 loss when stop was triggered intraday. The chart is already exhausted going into a binary earnings event with meaningful downside risk (beat-and-lower on tariff-impacted Q2 guidance). Per strategy rules: catalyst exists but the technical setup is poor — this is an explicit neutral condition. Entry would require a post-earnings gap-and-hold confirmation or a meaningful pullback to the $328-$335 hourly support zone before next catalyst.
